danger_signals.visual_form: Sprite animation and movement patterns in world view
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental threats; hostile entities require combat engagement

danger_signals.visual_form: NPC behavior changes and attack initiation
danger_signals.meaning: Player has violated faction code or is recognized as enemy to group

danger_signals.visual_form: Tileset variation showing obstacles or damaging terrain
danger_signals.meaning: Puzzle elements or physical dangers that block movement or require item manipulation

danger_signals.visual_form: NPC dialogue trees and world state changes reflecting conspiracy
danger_signals.meaning: Plot-critical danger: schemes against legitimate rule

safety_signals.visual_form: Clustered NPCs, building structures, dialogue availability
safety_signals.meaning: Rest points; merchant and quest hub locations

safety_signals.visual_form: Companion party members visible in world; allied faction members
safety_signals.meaning: Reduced threat; access to healing, equipment, quest information

safety_signals.visual_form: NPC with dialogue tree keywords; interactive containers and objects
safety_signals.meaning: Narrative progression points; safe interaction zones

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Royal guard uniforms and insignia (inferred from medieval fantasy setting)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemies wear opposing colors/garb when faction conflict erupts
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Castle, noble strongholds, official structures show crown authority

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: The Black Gate itself serves as visual and narrative symbol of threat
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Cultists and plot-aligned NPCs identifiable through dialogue and behavior
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hidden locations, corruption markers in world state, NPC behavioral changes reflecting conspiracy

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Monster sprite variants by type (humanoid, beast, magical)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Different sprite sets for each monster faction/type
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Infested areas show high monster density; lairs and dungeons

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Early game - Britannia stable but threatened
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: NPCs conduct normal routines; buildings intact; light monster presence in frontier areas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs follow daily schedules; merchants operate; quests available from authorities

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: NPC dialogue trees expand with plot keywords; suspicious characters appear; faction alignment becomes visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs change routines based on plot state; some flee or hide; quest-givers offer escalating missions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased monster sprite density in specific regions; environmental decay (inferred from 'monster infestations' narrative)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat frequency increases; NPCs avoid dangerous areas; some settlements show fear responses

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: World environment shows signs of corruption; key locations change appearance; faction forces mobilize visually
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All NPCs react to gate's presence through dialogue; combat becomes unavoidable; party composition critical

environmental_storytelling.example: Bread can be baked, weapons forged, instruments played, paintings created by manipulating objects - shows civilian life and culture
Drawers, backpacks, and scrolls displayed on-screen show character possessions and world detail through object density
Characters follow daily schedules; their presence in locations tells story of settlements and social structure

environmental_storytelling.technique: Dialogue tree keywords earned through plot
environmental_storytelling.example: Keywords only appear after learning them in-game, preventing player foreknowledge - reveals story progressively through visual dialogue interface
Objects stack to create stairs and puzzle solutions - environmental design communicates intent and narrative challenges
Castle and noble stronghold placement shows legitimate rule presence; absence in corrupted areas suggests conspiracy success

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Controlled by player; dialogue-driven interaction; can pick up and manipulate any non-nailed, non-heavy object
AI-controlled with behavior settings (aggressive, defensive, ranged); may swap weapons autonomously; occasionally drop weapons when retreating
Follow Avatar; participate in interactive tasks; inventory can be accessed via double-click for equipment management
Visible in settlements; conduct daily routines; offer dialogue with limited keyword options
Dialogue trees expand with suspicious keywords; behavior changes upon plot discovery; some flee or become hostile
Sprite animation indicates attack readiness; position and movement show threat level and tactical intelligence
Increased patrol frequency; visual mobilization in key areas; combat initiation based on faction alignment

notes: Ultima VII represents a paradigm shift in world-state communication via removal of grid-based tiles from player perception (retained internally), implementation of mouse-driven gumps for container visualization, and real-time world state tied to dialogue tree progression. The game's visual communication relies heavily on NPC behavioral manifestation (daily routines, dialogue expansion, combat response) rather than overt visual effects. Environmental storytelling occurs through interactive object manipulation, settlement density, and faction-associated location markers. Danger is communicated through monster sprite density, NPC avoidance patterns, and dialogue tree expansion of conspiracy-related keywords. Safety is signaled by settlement presence, merchant availability, and quest-giver accessibility. The Black Gate itself serves as the central visual/narrative symbol of the existential threat.
